name: mfthree
documentation:
# Matter and Form THREE Library

## Overview
The Matter and Form THREE library provides a comprehensive API for controlling and interacting with the Matter and Form THREE scanner. This library allows developers to build custom integrations, automate tasks, and create new front-end systems for 3D scanning.

## Project Home
[Github Project HERE](https://github.com/Matter-and-Form/three-python-library).

## Examples 

### Using Premade Examples
To connect to the scanner, you can use the provided examples. For instance, to run the connection example, execute:

```sh
python examples/connect.py
```

### Simple Example
Here is an example of how to use the library to connect to the scanner and control the projector:

```python
from matter_and_form_three import Scanner

# Create and connect to the scanner
scanner = Scanner(OnTask=None, OnMessage=None, OnBuffer=None)
# Use the Zeroconf address or replace with the ip
scanner.Connect("ws://matterandform.local:8081")

# Simple request to list all projects
projectTask = scanner.list_projects()

# Check the output from the task for errors
if projectTask.Error:
    print('Error:', projectTask.Error)
    return
# Do something with the output
for project_obj in projectTask.Output:
    project = Project.Brief(**project_obj)
    print('Project index:', project.index, ' - Name:', project.name)
```

### More Examples
The library comes with several pre-made examples. You can find them in the [examples directory](https://github.com/Matter-and-Form/three-python-library/tree/develop/three/examples).

To run a specific example, use:

```sh
python three/examples/<example_name>.py
```

## Documentation
For detailed documentation, visit the TODO -> [official documentation](https://github.com/Matter-and-Form/three-python-library/wiki).


## License
This project is licensed under the MIT License. See the [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

classes.name: MF.V3.Descriptors.Calibration.CaptureTarget
documentation:
Calibration capture target.

The camera calibration capture targets are used to draw quad overlays on the video stream to guide a user as to where to position the calibration card for each capture during camera calibration.

methods.name: MF.V3.Descriptors.Calibration.DetectedCard.Target.__init__
documentation:
A normalized value indicating how closely the calibration card matches the target
overlay. 0 indicates a poor match.  1 indicates a good match.

methods.name: MF.V3.Settings.Advanced.Advanced.PhaseFilter.__init__
documentation:
The filter kernel radius.

A neighboring value must be within this radius to be included in the filter.
If the kernel radius is set to zero, the phase filtering is disabled.

inner_classes.name: MF.V3.Settings.Advanced.Advanced.AdaptiveSampling
documentation:
Adaptive sampling settings

Adaptive sampling will downsample points in regions of low detail
and keep points in regions of high detail.

inner_classes.name: MF.V3.Settings.Scan.Scan.Processing.PhaseEdgeDetection
documentation:
Phase edge detection settings.

Phase edge detection produces a binary mask indicating the edges of a horizontal/vertical pair of phase images.  Since flat geometries give a constant phase image gradient, we use the second derivative (Laplacian) of the phase image to detect edges rather than the gradient.

The edge mask generated by phase edge detection is an input to both phase filtering and adaptive sampling.  If neither of these are enabled, the phase edge detection settings have no effect on the output point cloud.

methods.name: MF.V3.Settings.Scan.Scan.Processing.PhaseFilter.__init__
documentation:
The filter kernel radius.
A neighboring value must be within this radius to be included in the filter.
If the kernel radius is set to zero, the phase filtering is disabled.

methods.name: MF.V3.Settings.AutoFocus.AutoFocus.__init__

Apply the final focus value to both cameras.
This setting is ignored if more than one camera is selected.

methods.name: MF.V3.Settings.NewGroup.NewGroup.__init__

The index of the parent group in which the new group is created.
If unspecified the new group is added to the root of the group tree.

documentation: Apply settings to the current open project.

functions.name: MF.V3.Three.transform_group
documentation: Apply a rigid transformation to a group.

documentation: Auto focus one or both cameras.

methods.name: three.MF.V3.Settings.AutoFocus.AutoFocus.__init__
documentation:
Apply the final focus value to both cameras.
This setting is ignored if more than one camera is selected.

methods.name: three.MF.V3.Settings.NewGroup.NewGroup.__init__
documentation:
The index of the parent group in which the new group is created.
If unspecified the new group is added to the root of the group tree.

classes.name: three.scanner.Scanner

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__init__
documentation:
Initializes the Scanner object.

Args:
    * OnTask (Optional[Callable[[Task], None]]): Callback for any task related messages, default is None. Will fire for task complete, progress, and error.
    * OnMessage (Optional[Callable[[str], None]]): Callback function to handle messages, default is None. Messages are any calls related to the system that are not tasks or buffers. Eg. {"Message":{"HasTurntable":true}}
    * OnBuffer (Optional[Callable[[Any, bytes], None]]): Callback Function to handle buffer data, default is None. Buffers can be image or scan data. These are binary formats that are sent separately from tasks. Buffers will contain a header that describes the buffer data size. Please note that websocket buffers are limited in size and need to be sent in chunks.

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.Connect
documentation:
Attempts to connect to the scanner using the specified URI and timeout.

Args:
    * URI (str): The URI of the websocket server.
    * timeoutSec (int): Timeout in seconds, default is 5.

Returns:
    bool: True if connection is successful, raises Exception otherwise.

Raises:
    Exception: If connection fails within the timeout or due to an error.
Dynamically adds functions from the three_methods module to the Scanner class.
for name, func in inspect.getmembers(Three, predicate=inspect.isfunction):

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.IsConnected
documentation:
Checks if the scanner is currently connected.

Returns:
    bool: True if connected, False otherwise.

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__OnOpen
documentation:
Callback function for when the websocket connection is successfully opened.

Prints a success message to the console.

Args:
    ws: The websocket object.
Called when the connection is opened

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__OnClose
documentation:
Callback function for when the websocket connection is closed.

Prints a disconnect message to the console.

Args:
    ws: The websocket object.
    close_status_code: The code indicating why the websocket was closed.
    close_msg: Additional message about why the websocket was closed.
Called when the connection is closed

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__OnError
documentation:
Callback function for when an error occurs in the websocket connection.

Prints an error message to the console and stores the error for reference.

Args:
    ws: The websocket object.
    error: The error that occurred.

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__OnMessage
documentation:
Callback function for handling messages received via the websocket.

Determines the type of message received (Task, Buffer, or general Message) and
triggers the corresponding handler function if one is set.

Args:
    ws: The websocket object.
    message: The raw message received, which can be either a byte string or a JSON string.
Called when a message arrives on the connection

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.SendTask
documentation:
Sends a task to the scanner.
Tasks are general control requests for the scanner. (eg. Camera exposure, or Get Image)

Creates a task, serializes it, and sends it via the websocket.

Args:
    * task (Task): The task to send.
    * buffer (bytes): The buffer data to send, default is None.

Returns:
    Any: The task object that was sent.

Raises:
    AssertionError: If the connection is not established.

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__SendTask
documentation: Send a task to the scanner

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.__SendTaskWithBuffer
documentation: Send a task with its buffer to the scanner

documentation: Pop and restore scanner settings from the settings stack.

documentation: Push the current scanner settings to the settings stack.

documentation: Download the raw scan data for a scan in the current open project.

methods.name: three.scanner.Scanner.upload_project
documentation: Upload a project to the scanner.

Main class to manage and communicate with the Matter and Form THREE 3D Scanner via websocket.

Attributes:
    * OnTask (Optional[Callable[[Task], None]]): Callback for any task related messages, default is None. Will fire for task complete, progress, and error.
    * OnMessage (Optional[Callable[[str], None]]): Callback function to handle messages, default is None. Messages are any calls related to the system that are not tasks or buffers. Eg. {"Message":{"HasTurntable":true}}
    * OnBuffer (Optional[Callable[[Any, bytes], None]]): Callback Function to handle buffer data, default is None. Buffers can be image or scan data. These are binary formats that are sent separately from tasks. Buffers will contain a header that describes the buffer data size. Please note that websocket buffers are limited in size and need to be sent in chunks.
